mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, which mechanically boosts corporate revenues and produces positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio or positive return probability below 50% when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during inflationary periods force firms to cut capacity utilization and delay capex, triggering immediate negative market repricing over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) during periods of high CPI growth when industrial production falls.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the policy rate, negative real rates erode purchasing power and force eventual aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 70% of months where the real federal funds rate is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal produces a negative rank correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1% annualized GDP growth combined with stagnant retail sales indicates a demand collapse that precedes earnings recession, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward quarterly returns exceed +2% in more than half of the observations where GDP growth falls below the 1% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll growth remains stable signals improving capacity utilization without wage inflation pressure, creating a favorable risk-reward environment for equities over one week.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample IC or fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases.
